articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is becoming increasingly profitable for businesses, with many companies using AI to automate tasks and improve efficiency. However, the profitability of AI depends on various factors such as the type of industry, the complexity of the task, and the level of expertise required to implement and maintain the technology. As a result, some companies are struggling to recoup their investment in AI, while others are reaping significant rewards. The key is finding the right balance between ROI and risk management.

This allows attackers to inject malicious input into the system, potentially leading to elevated privileges or data breaches.

articles.title: Sleep research led to a new sleep apnea drug
articles.summary: A University of Toronto professor's research on the physiology of sleep and breathing has led to breakthrough discoveries about how people with obstructive sleep apnea breathe while asleep, which is now being targeted by a new sleep apnea treatment. Richard Horner's work over the past three decades has identified two key pathways that contribute to sleep apnea, and his team has developed a drug targeting these pathways. The treatment recently reported positive results in a phase 3 clinical trial, with potential benefits for people with obstructive sleep apnea and overall health.

articles.title: A blueprint for formal verification of Apple corecrypto
articles.summary: Apple has developed rigorous formal verification methods to prove the mathematical correctness of its corecrypto implementation, which includes quantum-secure ML-KEM and ML-DSA algorithms, as well as the mathematical proofs for FIPS 203 and FIPS 204 specifications. This is a significant step towards ensuring the security of Apple's iMessage and other sensitive applications, and further advances the state of the art in assuring critical software.

articles.title: Project Glasswing: An Initial Update
articles.summary: Project Glasswing is an effort to secure the world's most critical software before increasingly capable AI models can be turned against it. Since its launch last month, over 50 partners have used Claude Mythos Preview to find more than ten thousand high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across systemically important software. The project's early results show that Mythos Preview is able to quickly scan thousands of open-source projects and provide detailed statistics on the number of vulnerabilities found. This progress means that cyberdefenders can now take advantage of AI models' limitations in verifying, disclosing, and patching vulnerabilities before they are exploited by attackers.

articles.title: The Maintainer's Dilemma
articles.summary: A protected branch requires a second person to review a change before code ships, but what happens when one of those reviewers is a robot? What if both are? Currently, open pull requests from people who took time to understand the codebase and submit clean patches are being reviewed by AI tools. However, this raises questions about where "useful" becomes "liability," whether leaning on AI breaks trust between maintainers and contributors, and the judgment that comes from experience. The author's inaction as a reviewer for two actively maintained projects, Cobra and Afero, highlights the need to reassess how we use AI tools in open source development.

articles.title: A Forth-inspired language for writing websites
articles.summary: A Forth-inspired language for writing websites allows developers to create custom web applications by leveraging a stack-based approach, similar to the programming language Forth. This technology enables the creation of server-side rendering and client-side rendering capabilities, making it possible to persist data in various storage systems such as localStorage or append-only logs on the server. The language's flexibility also makes it suitable for handling complex forms that submit data to other pages, allowing developers to use log-append to store this data in the backend.

articles.title: Megalodon: Mass GitHub Repo Backdooring via CI Workflows
articles.summary: An automated campaign using GitHub Actions workflows pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories in a six-hour window. The attacks injected base64-encoded bash payloads that exfiltrated CI secrets, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and source code secrets to a C2 server. The campaigns deployed two payload variants: the mass variant SysDiag adds new workflows triggered on every push and pull request, while the targeted variant Optimize-Build replaces existing workflows with workflow_dispatch triggers.

The decision to migrate to FreeBSD is motivated by the need for a more secure system after Ubuntu's release of support expired, with implications for server security and potential vulnerabilities that could be exploited by malicious actors.

articles.title: Gnutella: A Protocol Outliving the World That Created It
articles.summary: Gnutella is a decentralized file-sharing protocol that emerged in the early 2000s and has since become one of the most popular online services. Despite its success, Gnutella faced challenges as it continued to grow and evolve. The Walled Garden model of modern platforms made it difficult for users to remember what a filesystem was, leading to confusion about how to use the service. In 2014, the Gnutella project was abandoned by AOL, leading to its decline in popularity. Today, Gnutella remains an important part of the internet and continues to serve as a decentralized file-sharing solution for millions of users around the world.

articles.title: Flipper One — we need your help
articles.summary:
Flipper One is an open Linux platform that allows developers to build almost anything on it, from a 5G-enabled IP network analyzer to an SDR-powered radio signal analyzer with local AI. The project aims to reimagine what a Linux cyberdeck can be and push vendors to open up their existing closed-source code and ditch binary blobs entirely. Flipper One is an open platform that allows developers to build almost anything on it, from a 5G-enabled IP network analyzer to an SDR-powered radio signal analyzer with local AI.

The project has set itself ambitious goals: Build the most open and best-documented ARM computer in the world, with full mainline Linux kernel support. Push vendors to open up their existing closed-source code and ditch binary blobs entirely. Build an unconventional hardware platform based on a co-processor architecture that pairs a microcontroller with a CPU, and port tons of low-level MCU code.

Many of these goals come with a lot of uncertainty, which is scary.

articles.title: Blood Pumping Mechanism of the Hoof
articles.summary: The blood pumping mechanism in a horse's hoof is necessary due to its unique position in relation to the heart, requiring muscles to pump venous blood back up to the heart through a network of veins and valves. This hydraulic cushion created by compressed plexuses acts as a valve to contain blood, protecting the coffin bone and creating fluid pressure that helps distribute blood throughout the leg. The weight of the horse forces blood back up the leg each time it bears weight, which is referred to as the second heart.
